XINPUT_CAPABILITIES

The XINPUT_CAPABILITIES structure, returned by the XInputGetCapabilities function, describes the capabilities of a connected Xbox peripheral.

typedef struct _XINPUT_CAPABILITIES
{
    BYTE    SubType;
    WORD    Reserved;
    union  
    {
      XINPUT_GAMEPAD Gamepad;
    } In;
    union
    {
      XINPUT_RUMBLE Rumble;
    } Out;
} XINPUT_CAPABILITIES, *PXINPUT_CAPABILITIES;

Members

SubType
This value indicates the specific type of the game controller being queried. The subtype will be one of the following values:
SubtypeMeaning
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_ARCADE_STICKArcade style joystick
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_DIGITAL_ARCADE_STICKArcade style joystick, and buttons that are normally analog instead return 0 or 255 only
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_FISHING_RODFishing rod controller
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_FLIGHT_STICKJoystick designed for flight games
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_GAMEPADGamepad
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_GAMEPAD_ALTGamepad, alternate configuration
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_LIGHTGUNLightgun
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_RADIO_FLIGHT_CONTROLWireless flight controller
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_SNOWBOARDSnowboard controller
XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_WHEELWheel

Note   See the Remarks section below for restrictions on the use of this subtype value. Additional subtypes may be added in the future.

Reserved
Reserved for internal use only.
Gamepad
Contains a structure of type XINPUT_GAMEPAD to describe available controller features and resolution.
Rumble
Contains a structure of type XINPUT_RUMBLE to describe available vibration functionality and resolution.

Remarks

The XINPUT_CAPABILITIES structure is returned by XInputGetCapabilities to indicate the characteristics and available functionality of a specified peripheral device.

The structures in the In and Out unions are the same packet structures used when performing actual input or output to the device. On return from XInputGetCapabilities, each bit is set that corresponds to a real input or feedback on the device. For binary state controls, such as digital buttons, the corresponding bit reflects whether or not the control is present. For proportional controls, such as thumbsticks, the bits also indicate the bit resolution (some number of the least significant bits may not be set).

The SubType member indicates the specific subtype of controller present. Games may detect the controller subtype and tune their handling of controller input or output based on subtypes that are well suited to their game genre. For example, a car racing game may want to check for the presence of a wheel controller to provide finer control of the car being driven. However, titles must not disable or ignore a device based on its subtype—subtypes not recognized by the game or for which the game is not specifically tuned should be treated as a standard gamepad (XINPUT_DEVSUBTYPE_GC_GAMEPAD). There are technical certification requirements associated with the correct treatment of such controller subtypes. There are additional technical certification requirements related to the mapping of controller features to game functionality. Please reference the Xbox Guide for more information.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xbox.h, for include use Xtl.h.
Import Library: Use Xapilib.lib

See Also

Input Device Structures | XINPUT_GAMEPAD, XINPUT_RUMBLE, XInputGetCapabilities
